# aipm

> A suggest-only work bot. It watches GitHub issues and PRs and Slack threads, spots who owes an action or where a thread has gone stale, and drafts a targeted nudge to the right person's Slack DM. It keeps a per-thread working-notes summary current. The machine never acts alone: it drafts, a human approves with one reaction.

aipm is a Cloudflare-native bot. The engine is platform-neutral; GitHub and Slack are adapters behind a common interface, and the LLM provider is an adapter too. Detection of *that* an action is owed is plain logic over the thread timeline; the LLM is used only for judgment (did this reply answer the question?), notes summarization, and nudge wording.

- Status: workers · cron · queues · d1, v0.1
- Site: https://aipm.dev
- Full reference for LLMs and agents: https://aipm.dev/llms-full.txt

## Principles

- suggest-only: The bot owns and edits its own artifacts (its working-notes comment, its DMs). Anything touching human-authored content is a proposal approved with one reaction.
- low-noise: One nudge per (person, thread, signal) per quiet period, then a digest. Mute/snooze always wins.
- deterministic: Detecting that an action is owed is plain logic over the timeline. The model only judges if a reply answered and words the nudge.
- shadow-first: Compute everything, post nothing, log what it would do. Enable posting one capability at a time.

## Engine pipeline

Ingest → Evaluate → Synthesize → Route → Aggregate, decoupled by Queues so LLM work is bounded and retryable. A Durable Object per cluster (or per thread) serializes updates so concurrent events can't double-nudge.

- Ingest: webhook or sweep → adapter normalizes → upsert Thread/Link/participants in D1. KV holds the webhook delivery-id for dedupe.
- Evaluate: deterministic signal detectors over the timeline + meta + state. No LLM. Emits/clears Signals.
- Synthesize: (LLM) update WorkingNotes; re-post only when the contentHash changes.
- Route: open Signals → Nudges; apply Preferences, choose channel by priority, enforce dedupe/backoff. (LLM only for wording.)
- Aggregate: per-person digests and a cluster-notes rollup.

## Signals (V1)

All thresholds are configuration. Quiet period is business-day-aware.

| Signal | Trigger | Target | Quiet | Channel |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| @mentioned, no response | webhook | mentioned person | 1 business day | DM (high pri) / digest |
| review requested | webhook | reviewer | 1 business day | DM |
| unaddressed review comments | webhook | PR author | 1 business day | DM |
| PR open, no reviewer | webhook + sweep | author | 4h | DM |
| draft PR aged | sweep | author | > 7 days | digest |
| in-progress stale | sweep | owner/assignee | > N days | digest (DM if high pri) |
| blocker cleared | webhook | blocked thread's owner | immediate | DM |

When a thread reaches a terminal state (closed/merged/done), all its signals clear. One nudge per dedupeKey per quiet period; after N escalations a signal drops to digest-only. Authors whose login ends in [bot] are never nudge targets.
